description_long: This is a sleek, modern helicopter with a streamlined, matte-black fuselage and a sharply angled tail boom. The helicopter features a large, angular cockpit with multiple tinted windows, providing wide visibility for the crew. Its main rotor consists of four long, straight blades, and the tail section is equipped with a shrouded tail rotor for enhanced safety and noise reduction. The landing gear is composed of two minimalist, black skids that run parallel beneath the fuselage. The overall design suggests a high-performance, possibly military or advanced civilian utility helicopter, with no visible registration markings or external armaments.
description: A matte-black, streamlined helicopter with a large tinted cockpit, four-blade main rotor, shrouded tail rotor, and minimalist landing skids.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the angular cockpit windows, main rotor, and both landing skids clearly.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail boom, shrouded tail rotor, and the tapering fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the tail section, shrouded tail rotor, and the length of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the cockpit windows, main rotor, and the right landing skid.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored black with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and appear to be of equal length, extending well beyond the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Shrouded tail rotor with multiple short blades, oriented horizontally within a protective circular housing at the end of the tail boom. The blades and housing are black, with no visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, minimalist landing skids made of black metal or composite, running the length of the lower fuselage. No visible crossbars or footpads are present.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows with a dark green tint, wrapping around the front and sides of the cockpit. The windows are framed by the fuselage with no visible markings.
